hypothesis: If CompGen employs a chemically informed autoencoder pretrained on the Materials Project and a conditional latent diffusion model conditioned on periodic-table representations, adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed elements (with OC20 pretraining and HEA fine-tuning), then it will generate shell-wise surface compositions with lower Fréchet Distance, lower Leakage, and higher Cosine Similarity than a chemically-agnostic baseline across varying data fractions, demonstrating superior distributional fidelity, constraint compliance, and data efficiency.
